opportunities.entry_trigger: Enter on pullbacks into 400-425 that hold above 385 (gap midpoint); add on any retest of 405-410 with volume confirmation on the daily chart.
opportunities.rationale: Micron gapped +14% on Mar 18 earnings (prev close ~370 to 441+ open), confirming a massive Q2 FY2026 beat driven by HBM AI demand. Retraced from 461 peak to 422 by Mar 20, holding above gap base at 370. Susquehanna PT $525 implies 24% upside. R:R from midpoint 412 is 1:2.3 to 475. Invalidated below 385 (gap fill below 50% level).

opportunities.entry_trigger: Enter Mon Mar 24 open if price holds above 23.50; this is a 1-day pre-earnings hold — EXIT by close Mar 24 or pre-market Mar 25 before AMC print. Alternatively, shift to gap follow-through if CCL beats and gaps above 26.
opportunities.rationale: Carnival reports Q1 FY2026 on Mar 25 (AMC). Beat 4/4 last quarters with avg surprise +18%. Consumer travel demand resilient despite macro headwinds — record booking levels through 2026. Price pulled back from 25.79 to 24.12 on broad market weakness (not company-specific), creating an oversold entry. MODERATE not HIGH because: (1) high-vol regime caps conviction, (2) only 1 day before earnings limits run-up thesis, (3) Iran conflict creates fuel cost uncertainty for cruise lines. Entry zone 23.50-25.00 (3.1% width). R:R from 24.25 midpoint: stop 22.00 (-9.3%), target 28.50 (+17.5%), R:R 1:1.9. Invalidated below 22.00.

opportunities.entry_trigger: Enter Mon-Tue Mar 24-25 in the 158-166 zone; exit by close Mar 26 (day before earnings). If price rallies >5% from entry, take partial profit and trail stop to breakeven.
opportunities.rationale: Lululemon reports Q4 FY2025 on Mar 27 (AMC). Beat 4/4 last quarters with accelerating international growth and strong holiday season reads. Price base-building at 157-166 range over 5 sessions after broad market selloff — not company-specific weakness. 5-day pre-earnings window aligns with HIGH conviction protocol. Beat history 4/4, RSI estimated ~42 (mid-range, recovering from oversold), no macro event within 24h of earnings (FOMC was Mar 19). Entry zone 158-166 (4.9% width). R:R from midpoint 162: stop 150 (-7.4%), target 185 (+14.2%), R:R 1:1.9. EXIT BEFORE Mar 27 print. Invalidated below 150.

opportunities.entry_trigger: Enter Mon Mar 24 in the 145-151 zone; exit by close Mar 25 (day before earnings). Paychex is a defensive compounder that typically rallies into prints during risk-off environments.
opportunities.rationale: Paychex reports Q3 FY2026 on Mar 26 (BMO). Beat 4/4 last quarters with consistent mid-single-digit revenue growth and expanding margins. Defensive payroll/HR services business benefits from sticky enterprise contracts — recession-resistant revenue model. High-vol regime favors defensive compounders with reliable beats. Beat history 4/4, RSI estimated ~48 (neutral), no macro event within 24h. Entry zone 145-151 (4.0% width). R:R from midpoint 148: stop 139 (-6.1%), target 165 (+11.5%), R:R 1:1.9. EXIT BEFORE Mar 26 print. Invalidated below 139.

opportunities.entry_trigger: Enter Mon Mar 24 open in the 180-188 zone; exit by close Mar 24 before the Mar 25 BMO print. Short holding window — size accordingly.

Business services compounder with 90%+ retention rates — defensive quality in high-vol regime. MODERATE not HIGH: (1) only 1-day pre-earnings window limits run-up potential, (2) one miss in last 4 quarters reduces beat confidence, (3) entry zone slightly wide at 4.3%. R:R from midpoint 184: stop 173 (-6.0%), target 205 (+11.4%), R:R 1:1.9. EXIT BEFORE Mar 25 BMO print. Invalidated below 173.

opportunities.entry_trigger: Enter on pullbacks toward 195-199 that hold above 185; target 225 (prior resistance zone and 200-day MA area).

Consolidated at 199.99 through Mar 20, holding the gap base. Enterprise IT spending bellwether signal is constructive. Regime headwind (high vol, VIX 26.78) caps conviction at MODERATE. R:R from midpoint 199 is 1:1.86 to 225. Invalidated below 185 (pre-earnings swing low).

opportunities.entry_trigger: Enter Mon Mar 24 in the 31.00-33.50 zone; exit by close Mar 25 (day before earnings). Pet spending is recession-resistant — look for confirmation of Autoship growth in the print.
opportunities.rationale: Chewy reports Q4 FY2025 on Mar 26 (AMC). Beat 3/4 last quarters with Autoship subscription revenue growing 15%+ YoY. Pet spending is defensive in downturns — pet owners prioritize animal care spending. Active customer growth re-accelerating after post-pandemic normalization. MODERATE: beat 3/4 (not 4/4), RSI estimated ~45 (neutral), no macro within 24h. Entry zone 31.00-33.50 (3.8% width on midpoint). R:R from midpoint 32.25: stop 29.00 (-10.1%), target 38.00 (+17.8%), R:R 1:1.8. EXIT BEFORE Mar 26 AMC print. Invalidated below 29.00.
